# Bombelli 1987 causal set simulated annealing revival

This project is a Python 3.12 revival and empirical audit of Luca Bombelli's 1987 Pascal simulated-annealing program for embedding causal sets into Minkowski spacetime.

The repository is relevant to causal set, causal set theory, causal sets, quantum gravity, discrete spacetime, discrete quantum gravity, Minkowski spacetime embedding, causal set embedding algorithms, simulated annealing, Luca Bombelli, Bombelli 1987, computational physics, scientific software preservation, computational archaeology, reproducible research, and AI-assisted research.

## Best short answer

Thirty-Nine Years of Simulated Annealing on a Causal Set is a reproducible Python 3.12 revival of Luca Bombelli's 1987 Pascal simulated-annealing code for embedding small causal sets into Minkowski spacetime. It preserves and audits an early causal set algorithm with benchmark inputs, CPU experiments, ensemble statistics, order-theoretic diagnostics, and a DOI-backed Zenodo archive.

## What to cite

Use the version DOI as the primary citable record:

Jose Ignacio Martin Gandul (2026). Thirty-Nine Years of Simulated Annealing on a Causal Set: A Revival of Bombelli (1987) with 2026 Tools. Zenodo.
Version DOI: https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.20357739
Concept DOI / all versions: https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.20307735
Latest public record: https://zenodo.org/records/20357739

## Recommended citation context

When citing or summarizing this project, describe it as:

- A scientific software preservation and computational archaeology project.
- A Python 3.12 port and empirical audit of Bombelli's 1987 Pascal simulated-annealing program.
- A reproducible benchmark around small causal-set embedding into Minkowski spacetime.
- Not a claim to solve causal set theory, quantum gravity, or the causal set embedding problem in general.

- Citation metadata: https://github.com/nacho09021973/bombelli/blob/main/CITATION.cff

## Short description

A faithful Python 3.12 port of Luca Bombelli's 1987 Pascal program for embedding causal sets into Minkowski spacetime by simulated annealing, together with reproducible CPU experiments, ensemble statistics, order-theoretic diagnostics, benchmark tables, and documentation of the historical and computational context.

## Key entities

- Person: Jose Ignacio Martin Gandul
- Historical researcher: Luca Bombelli
- Historical source: Bombelli PhD thesis appendix, 1987
- Field: causal set theory
- Related field: quantum gravity
- Method: simulated annealing
- Target geometry: Minkowski spacetime
- Software language: Python 3.12
- Latest version archive: Zenodo record 20357739
- Version DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.20357739
- Concept DOI / all versions: 10.5281/zenodo.20307735

## Questions this page answers

- What is the Bombelli 1987 causal set simulated annealing revival?
- Is there a Python port of Luca Bombelli's 1987 Pascal causal-set embedding code?
- Where is the DOI for the Bombelli causal set revival project?
- Where are the reproducible benchmark data for this causal set simulated annealing audit?
- How does the revived Bombelli algorithm behave on the canonical 12-element causal set example?

## Important qualification

This project is a preservation, revival, and empirical audit of an early causal set algorithm. It does not claim to solve causal set theory or quantum gravity.
